raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-reported commitment to dialectical openness and avoidance of polarized cognition",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "all-or-nothing frames are recognizable mental states that can be transcended",
    "opposing positions typically contain partial truths worth seeking",
    "willingness to locate those partial truths is both possible and valuable"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a meta-cognitive stance; presupposes a pluralistic ontology of truth without logical contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "reality contains genuinely distributed partial truths across apparent opposites, and the practitioner can reliably distinguish all-or-nothing framing from appropriate categorical judgments",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "some domains contain non-negotiable binary truths where seeking 'partial truth on either side' produces false equivalence or evasion of decisive reality"
}
